text: Competitive and market performance risks are evident in Apple's significant underperformance versus the broader market. Over the past year, Apple returned -5.80% compared to the S&P 500's +13.56%, representing a -19.36% excess return deficit. This substantial underperformance indicates investors are rotating away from Apple relative to market benchmarks. The company faces ongoing pressure in key markets including China, where competitive dynamics with local smartphone manufacturers remain intense. While services growth continues to drive investor interest according to analyst reports, the core hardware business faces mounting competitive threats that could pressure future margins and market share.
